word_list.word: a man who cannot tolerate small misfortunes, can never accomplish great things
word_list.pronunciation: 蓯 m藞忙n h藢u藧 k忙n藞蓱藧t t藞蓱藧l蓺晒藢e瑟t sm藞蓴藧l m瑟sf藞蓴藧晒t蕛蓹nz k忙n n藞蓻v蓺晒 蓯k藞蓱藧mpl瑟蕛 伞晒藞e瑟t 胃藞瑟艐z

senses.definition: used to imply that individuals who are unable to handle small setbacks or misfortunes will not be able to accomplish great things in life

word_list.pronunciation: b藞蓻晒 忙nd f藞蓴藧晒b蓻晒

senses.definition: used to encourage individuals to show patience, tolerance, and endurance while dealing with difficult situations or people, emphasizing the importance of acceptance over resistance

examples.x: Success often requires us to bear and forbear through various challenges and setbacks.

word_list.word: bear with evil and expect good
word_list.pronunciation: b藞蓻晒 w瑟冒 藞i藧v蓹l 忙nd 蓻ksp藞蓻kt 伞藞蕣d

senses.definition: used to imply that one should remain patient and endure difficult or negative situations with the hope or expectation that positive outcomes or results will eventually follow

examples.x: My grandfather always said the secret to a happy life is learning to bear with evil and expect good, no matter what difficulties arise.

word_list.pronunciation: 藞i藧v蓹n 蓯 w藞蓽藧m w瑟l 蕛藢忙l w蕣d t藞蓽藧n

senses.definition: used to imply that all people will eventually get frustrated or fed up if they are continually wronged, even those who are very timid or obedient

examples.x: The employees who were constantly mistreated and overworked finally stood up to their boss, showing that even a worm will turn when pushed too far.

word_list.word: if you cannot stand the heat, get out of the kitchen
word_list.pronunciation: 瑟f ju藧 k忙n藞蓱藧t st藞忙nd 冒蓹 h藞i藧t 伞蓻t 藢a蕣删蓹v 冒蓹 k藞瑟t蕛蓹n

senses.definition: used to imply that if someone is unable to handle the pressure or difficulties of a situation, they should remove themselves from it, emphasizing the importance of perseverance and avoiding excuses

word_list.word: it is a long lane that has no turning
word_list.pronunciation: 瑟t 瑟z 蓯 l藞蓱藧艐 l藞e瑟n 冒忙t h蓯z n藞o蕣 t藞蓽藧n瑟艐

senses.definition: used to emphasize that no matter how challenging or prolonged a situation may seem, there is always the possibility of change or improvement, encouraging individuals to persevere

examples.x: After months of job searching, the recent graduate was about to give up hope, but remembering that it is a long lane that has no turning encouraged her to keep trying, and eventually she landed her dream job.

word_list.word: oaks may fall when reeds stand the storm
word_list.pronunciation: 藞o蕣ks m藞e瑟 f藞蓴藧l w蓻n 晒藞i藧dz st藞忙nd 冒蓹 st藞o藧晒m

senses.definition: used to suggest that adaptability and flexibility can sometimes be more advantageous than strength and power in overcoming challenges and surviving crises

examples.x: The business that was able to pivot and adjust its strategy during the pandemic survived, while larger and more established companies failed, demonstrating that oaks may fall when reeds stand the storm.

word_list.word: sticks and stones may break my bones
word_list.pronunciation: st藞瑟ks 忙nd st藞o蕣nz m藞e瑟 b晒藞e瑟k ma瑟 b藞o蕣nz

senses.definition: used to imply that physical violence or insults cannot harm a person's inner self, encouraging individuals to be resilient and not let negativity or criticism bring them down

word_list.word: what cannot be cured (must|should) be endured
word_list.pronunciation: w藢蕦t k忙n藞蓱藧t bi藧 kj藞蕣晒d m藞蕦st 蕛藢蕣d bi藧 蓻nd藞蕣晒d

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of accepting and enduring circumstances that cannot be changed or resolved

word_list.word: cry with one eye and laugh with the other
word_list.pronunciation: k晒藞a瑟 w瑟冒 w藞蕦n 藞a瑟 忙nd l藞忙f w瑟冒冒瑟 藞蕦冒蓺

senses.definition: used to highlight the concept of emotional resilience, being able to acknowledge and experience different emotions without being overwhelmed by any single one

examples.x: As a single mother, she had to learn to cry with one eye and laugh with the other, balancing the challenges of raising her children alone with the joy of watching them grow and thrive.

word_list.pronunciation: l藞蓻t 冒藢蓻m l藞忙f 冒忙t w藞瑟n

senses.definition: used to suggest that individuals who are focused on their goals should not be discouraged by the laughter or ridicule of others but should instead continue to pursue their goals with determination

word_list.pronunciation: h藞蓱藧晒d w藞蓽藧dz b晒藞e瑟k n藞o蕣 b藞o蕣nz

senses.definition: used to encourage people to not take insults or criticism too personally, and to remember that words cannot physically harm them

examples.x: My coach can be tough on us during practice, but hard words break no bones - I know he's just pushing us to be our best on the field.
